title: ranunculus ophioglossifolius subsp. fontanus
intro: Ranunculus ophioglossifolius subsp. fontanus is a subspecies of the Ranunculus ophioglossifolius plant, commonly known as the adder's tongue water crowfoot or water crowfoot, which is a semi-aquatic perennial plant native to Europe. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, making it effective in treating wounds and infections. The plant's health benefits include its ability to reduce inflammation, promote wound healing, and exhibit antimicrobial activity, making it a valuable asset in traditional medicine. Herbal preparations of Ranunculus ophioglossifolius subsp. fontanus include infusions, decoctions, and topical applications, which can be used to treat various skin conditions and injuries.
